name: Raindrop Pro
price_basis: Pro at $2.99 monthly or $27.99 yearly, from the official App Store listing because raindrop.io renders prices in-app only, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KILL: barely $3 a month, but saving pages with full text search is native Zo behavior. You own this already.
what_zo_builds: A permanent bookmark library: a save route that fetches the page, stores a full copy in your workspace, tags it, and indexes every word for search. Collections are folders. A monthly job flags dead links. This is the rare kill where Zo is not imitating the product; page capture and full text search are things it does out of the box.
dont_kill_if: You share curated collections publicly or with a team and those inbound links have an audience. A personal archive does not replace a published collection page.
build_brief: Goal: replace Raindrop Pro with a bookmark archive I own. Export all bookmarks with tags and collections. Import them into /bookmarks as folders, fetch a permanent copy of every saved page, and index the text for search. Add a save route for new links and a monthly job that flags dead URLs. Spot-check search against Raindrop on the same queries before switching. Done when every bookmark resolves to a local copy, search finds pages by their content, and the subscription is canceled.
